sections.title: Storage Categories
points: AWS storage is commonly divided into Object, Block, and File storage.
Object storage = scalable internet-accessible data storage.
Block storage = virtual hard drive volumes for servers.
File storage = shared folder-style storage using file systems.
sections.exam_tip: S3 = Object | EBS = Block | EFS/FSx = File

sections.title: Amazon S3 (Simple Storage Service)
points: Stores files as objects inside buckets.
Used for backups, static websites, logs, media, analytics data.
sections.exam_tip: Need scalable file/object storage? Think S3.

sections.title: Amazon S3 Key Features
points: Versioning protects against accidental deletion or overwrite.
Lifecycle policies automate movement to cheaper tiers.

sections.title: Amazon S3 Storage Classes
points: S3 Standard-IA = infrequent access, fast retrieval.
S3 One Zone-IA = lower cost, single AZ.
S3 Glacier Deep Archive = lowest cost long-term archive.

sections.title: Amazon EBS (Elastic Block Store)
points: Persistent block storage for Amazon EC2.
Acts like a virtual hard drive.
Low-latency storage for databases and applications.

sections.title: EBS Snapshots
points: Can restore new volumes from snapshots.

sections.title: EC2 Instance Store
points: Temporary storage physically attached to host server.
Lost when instance stops, fails, or terminates.
Used for buffers, cache, temporary data.

sections.title: Amazon EFS (Elastic File System)
points: Managed shared file storage for Linux workloads.
Multiple EC2 instances can mount at same time.

sections.title: Amazon FSx
points: Managed file systems for specific workloads.
FSx for Windows File Server = SMB + Active Directory.
Used when EFS is not the right fit.
sections.exam_tip: Windows file shares = FSx for Windows.

sections.title: EFS vs FSx
points: FSx Lustre = HPC / fast compute workloads.

sections.title: AWS Backup
sections.exam_tip: Many backups across services = AWS Backup.

sections.title: AWS Storage Gateway
points: Hybrid service connecting on-premises storage to AWS.
Lets local environments use AWS cloud storage.
Supports file, volume, and tape models.

sections.title: AWS Elastic Disaster Recovery
points: Continuous replication of on-prem or cloud servers to AWS.
sections.exam_tip: Need disaster failover to AWS = Elastic Disaster Recovery.

sections.title: Shared Responsibility Model (Storage)
points: Customer secures data, permissions, retention, backups, encryption choices.

sections.title: Common Exam Traps
points: S3 is object storage, not block storage.
EBS usually attaches to one instance.
Glacier is not for hot frequently accessed data.
sections.exam_tip: AWS often tests what a service is NOT.
